What people are proud of, even if it seems unimportant, feels like a crown to them; but pride leads to destruction. Drunkards act very foolishly! Those who give in to wine give in to Satan; and there is no greater hardship than heavy drinking. Their health is ruined; people fail in their jobs and lose everything they own, and their families are destroyed by it. Their souls are in danger of being lost forever, all just to satisfy a shameful desire. It’s even worse among God's followers, like Israel was, than with any other group. And he is right to take away the abundance they misuse. The abundance they were proud of is only a fleeting flower, like early fruit that is picked and eaten as soon as it appears.

The prophet then turns to Judah, whom he calls the remaining part of his people. Happy are those alone who boast in the Lord Almighty himself. From this, his people gain wisdom and strength for every task and every battle. But it is only through Christ Jesus that the holy God communicates with sinful people. And whether those who teach are drunk with wine or influenced by false teachings and ideas about the kingdom and salvation of the Messiah, they not only go astray themselves but lead many others wrong. All places where such people have taught are filled with errors. For our understanding of God’s things, it is necessary that the same instruction and the same explanation be repeated to us often so we can understand them better. God calls us to what is truly for our benefit by his word; serving God is the only true rest for those tired from serving sin, and there is no refreshment except under Christ's easy guidance. All of this had little effect on the people. Those who refuse to understand something that is clear, but scorn and reject it as unimportant, are justly punished. If we are at peace with God, we have effectively made an agreement with death; whenever it comes, it cannot harm us if we belong to Christ. But to think of making death our friend while by sin we make God our enemy is absurd. And do not those who trust in their own righteousness or wait for repentance on their deathbeds make lies their refuge? This is a decision to stop sinning when they are no longer able to do so.

Here is a promise of Christ as the only hope for escaping coming wrath. This foundation was laid in Zion, in God’s eternal plans. This foundation is a stone, strong and able to support his church. It is a tested stone, a chosen stone, approved by God, and has never failed anyone who has tried it. A cornerstone, binding together the entire building and bearing the whole weight; precious in the sight of the Lord and of every believer; a sure foundation on which to build. And whoever in any age or nation believes this testimony and rests all their hopes and their eternal soul on this foundation will never be ashamed. The proper result of faith in Christ is to quiet and calm the soul until events are timed by him who has all times in his own power. Whatever people trust in for justification, except the righteousness of Christ; or for wisdom, strength, and holiness, except the influence of the Holy Spirit; or for happiness, except God’s favor—that protection they thought to find will prove insufficient. Those who rest in their own righteousness will deceive themselves: the bed is too short, the covering too narrow. God will be glorified in carrying out his plans. If those who claim to be members of God's church act like Philistines and Canaanites, they must expect to be treated as such. So do not dare to mock God’s warnings or the approach of judgment.

The farmer applies himself to his work with care and planning in all its aspects according to their nature. Thus the Lord, who has given people this wisdom, is wonderful in counsel and excellent in working. As the situation requires, he threatens, corrects, spares, shows mercy, or executes vengeance. Troubles are God’s threshing tools, to separate us from the world, to remove what is worthless from us, and to prepare us for use. God will proportion them to our strength; they will not be heavier than necessary. When his purpose is fulfilled, the trials and sufferings of his people will end; his wheat will be gathered into storage, but the worthless parts will be burned with unquenchable fire.
